doc_name: Technical Report on Operating System Tracing Interface.pdf

structure.title: Preface
structure.summary:
The partial document is the published AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform R25-11 “Technical Report on Operating System Tracing Interface” (Doc-ID 1083).  
It presents the specification for an OS-level tracing interface (ARTI) that enables observation of adaptive-platform task and process events.  
Main points covered: document metadata, disclaimer/copyright, release-by-release change history, and a detailed table of contents outlining the scope, terminology, functional specification of the ARTI tracing interface (adapter management, task interface, process interface), and a comprehensive API specification with type definitions and sixteen callback notifications for task/process life-cycle and context-switch events.

structure.title: Introduction
structure.summary: The document introduces a low-level tracing API for the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform operating system, designed to record task and process events with minimal runtime impact. Its purpose is to enable precise timing analysis—CPU usage, deadlines, periodicity accuracy, and lock dependencies—by breaking down time consumption within the software. The API is targeted at driver-level use by stack and trace tool vendors, not application engineers, and explicitly excludes application or middleware-level tracing to avoid disturbing system behavior.

nodes.title: Objectives
nodes.summary: The partial document introduces a technical report on the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform’s Operating System Tracing Interface. It outlines the objective of providing a low-level API for tracing tasks and processes with minimal runtime impact, enabling analysis of timing requirements such as CPU usage, deadlines, and periodicity. The scope is limited to the adaptive platform’s OS level, targeting stack and trace tool vendors rather than application engineers, and emphasizes that tracing at application or middleware levels is avoided to prevent performance interference.

nodes.title: Scope
nodes.summary: The document introduces a low-level API for tracing tasks and processes in the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform operating system. Its main objectives are to enable minimal-overhead collection of timing data to verify CPU usage, deadlines, and periodicity, and to reveal timing dependencies and locks. The API is targeted at driver-level use by stack and trace-tool vendors, not application engineers, to avoid altering runtime behavior.

structure.title: Definition of terms and acronyms
structure.summary: The excerpt is a terminology section that lists and briefly defines the key acronyms and technical terms—such as Adaptive Application, Execution Management, Execution Manifest, Machine, Modelled Process, Operating System, Process, and Task—used throughout the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform R25-11 technical report on Operating-System tracing interfaces.

nodes.title: Acronyms and abbreviations
nodes.summary: The excerpt is a terminology section from an AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform technical report on OS tracing. It lists and defines the key acronyms and concepts—Adaptive Application, ARTI, Execution Management, Execution Manifest, Machine, Manifest, Modelled Process, Operating System, Process, Task—used throughout the document, cross-referencing the AUTOSAR Glossary and Execution Management specification for fuller definitions.

structure.title: Related Documentation
structure.summary:
The excerpt introduces the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform R25-11 “Technical Report on Operating System Tracing Interface.”  
It focuses on Section 4, the Functional Specification, which presents the ARTI (AUTOSAR Runtime Instrumentation) tracing interface.  
Key points:

- Purpose: enable understanding, verification and visualization of OS timing behavior.  
- Core component: the “OS/ARTI Adapter” – a standardized, lightweight C API layer inserted between the POSIX/OS and tracing tools.  
- Implementation: ARTI hooks are vendor-supplied; they may be C macros (collapsible to empty) or functions, allowing builds with or without tracing.  
- Two-level model: distinguishes “process” (mandatory container) and “task” (schedulable unit/thread) to align with AUTOSAR architecture.

structure.title: Functional Specification
structure.summary: The excerpt introduces the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform “OS/ARTI Adapter,” a standardized, lightweight C API layer that exposes OS-level trace hooks so timing behavior can be understood, verified, and visualized. It explains that the hooks are vendor-supplied (either macros or empty functions), sit inside an “OS/ARTI Driver,” and follow AUTOSAR’s two-level task/process model.

nodes.title: ARTI Tracing Interface
nodes.summary: The excerpt defines the OS/ARTI Adapter as the AUTOSAR AP trace-point layer inside the OS; it exposes a standardized, lightweight C API (ARTI hooks) that a tracing-tool vendor implements as macros or functions, allows null expansion when no tracer is present, and records task/thread and process events so tools can visualize and verify OS timing behavior.

nodes.title: OS/ARTI Adapter
nodes.summary:
The excerpt defines the AUTOSAR-ARTI OS-level tracing interface.  
It specifies the “OS/ARTI Adapter” that exposes standardized trace hooks so tools can capture, verify and visualize OS timing.  
Hooks may be implemented as C macros or functions; if no tracer is present they expand to no-ops.  
The interface adopts AUTOSAR’s two-level model: a Process contains multiple Tasks (threads).  
Each hook carries context parameters—callingContext (interrupt state), coreId, taskId, processId—whose numeric IDs are derived internally by the OS and can be mapped to human-readable names or to Adaptive-Platform modelled processes via the Execution Manifest.  
Adapter life-cycle starts with ArtiVersionInfo: the OS offers its highest supported API version; the tracer returns its own.  
Tracing proceeds only if the versions match; otherwise the OS may retry with a lower version or disable tracing.

nodes.title: Adapter Management
nodes.summary: The excerpt defines how the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform R25-11 OS-Tracing Interface (ARTI) represents tasks/processes via taskId/processId, explains that these IDs are mapped to OS-internal identifiers and can be correlated with model-level process names through Execution-Manifest data, and then details the Adapter life-cycle: mandatory version negotiation (ArtiVersionInfo) before initialization (ArtiInit) and cleanup (ArtiCleanup) of the tracing adapter, plus the minimal task state machine (Ready → Running/Suspended) that traced task events must follow.

nodes.title: Task Interface
nodes.summary:
The excerpt specifies the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform “ARTI” tracing interface for an operating system.  
It mandates:

- **Initialisation/Cleanup**: the OS/ARTI Adapter must call ArtiInit immediately after API-version confirmation and ArtiCleanup on stop, both supplying the current interrupt context.  

- **Task life-cycle hooks**: for every task state change the adapter must/should invoke the corresponding ARTI function with standard parameters (callingContext, coreId, taskId, plus processId/name where relevant):  
  – ArtiTaskCreate, ArtiTaskExit, ArtiTaskInfo (mandatory)  
  – ArtiTaskSwitch (mandatory on every transition into Running)  
  – ArtiTaskWait, ArtiTaskRelease, ArtiTaskPreempt, ArtiTaskRename (recommended)  

- **Task state model**: minimal Ready → Running → Waiting state machine is assumed; trace hooks for Ready↔Running are mandatory, those for Waiting are optional if the OS does not distinguish that state.

nodes.title: Process Interface
nodes.summary: The excerpt defines the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform “Process Interface” for OS tracing: whenever ARTI is enabled the OS/ARTI Adapter must/should invoke six hook functions—ArtiProcessSwitch, ArtiProcessCreate, ArtiProcessDestroy, ArtiProcessRename, and ArtiProcessInfo—at every process switch, creation, termination, renaming, or initial enumeration, passing standardized parameters (callingContext, coreId, processId, parentId, nextId, processName) so the trace collector can reconstruct the complete process life-cycle and affinity information.

structure.title: API Specification
structure.summary: The excerpt defines the AUTOSAR AP R25-11 Operating System Tracing Interface (ARTI) structure ArtiVersionInfoType. It specifies that this struct (declared in ara/log/osarti.h) contains two uint32_t members: apiVersion, which conveys the requested/supported API version (0x01 for this release), and buildVersion, which carries the informal build version of either the OS or the ARTI driver.

nodes.title: Type Definitions
nodes.summary:
The excerpt defines the AUTOSAR AP R25-11 ARTI (AUTOSAR Runtime Tracing Interface) version-information structure ArtiVersionInfoType.  
It specifies the struct’s purpose (to convey the ARTI version supported by the OS and its driver), its header files, and its two uint32_t members: apiVersion (set to 0x01 for this release) and buildVersion (informal driver/OS build identifier).

nodes.title: ArtiVersionInfoType
nodes.summary:
The excerpt is a fragment of the AUTOSAR AP R25-11 technical report that specifies the tracing-interface API.  
It concentrates on two type definitions in section 5.1:

1. ArtiVersionInfoType (5.1.1) – a C struct that conveys version and identity information between the OS and the ARTI driver.  
   - apiVersion: 32-bit field set to 0x01 for this release; used to negotiate/request the API version.  
   - buildVersion: 32-bit informal field carrying the build number of the OS or driver.  
   - productName: pointer to a C string giving the OS/driver product name.  
   - vendorName: pointer to a C string giving the OS/driver vendor name.

2. CallingContext (5.1.2) – an enumeration that tells hooks whether they run with interrupts already disabled (kInterruptsDisabled = 0) or may themselves disable interrupts (kInterruptsMayBeDisabled = 1).

All items are declared in ara/log/osarti.h and trace back to requirement RS_OSI_00210.

nodes.title: CallingContext
nodes.summary: The excerpt defines two version-info string fields (productName, vendorName) and an enumeration CallingContext that classifies interrupt states. It then specifies two OS-tracing callback functions—ArtiTaskSwitch and ArtiTaskWait—that the OS/ARTI adapter must invoke on every task switch or task wait, passing the core ID, task ID, and the CallingContext value to inform the tracer about interrupt safety.

nodes.title: Callback Notifications
nodes.summary: The excerpt defines two ART/OSTI hook functions—ArtiTaskSwitch and ArtiTaskWait—used to notify a tracer of task-state changes in an AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform system. Both hooks are reentrant, take a CallingContext flag indicating whether interrupts may be disabled, and are declared in “ara/log/osarti.h”. ArtiTaskSwitch reports when a new task becomes running on a given core, while ArtiTaskWait signals that the specified task is about to wait.

nodes.title: ArtiTaskSwitch
nodes.summary:
The excerpt defines two OS-tracing callback hooks—ArtiTaskSwitch and ArtiTaskWait—specified in the AUTOSAR AP R25-11 Operating-System Tracing Interface.  
Both hooks are declared in “ara/log/osarti.h”, take a CallingContext parameter that indicates whether interrupts may be disabled (kInterruptsMayNotBeDisabled=2), and are reentrant.  
ArtiTaskSwitch notifies a tracer that coreId is switching to task nextId; ArtiTaskWait notifies that taskId on coreId is entering the waiting state.

nodes.title: ArtiTaskWait
nodes.summary: The excerpt is a technical specification (AUTOSAR AP R25-11) that defines four OS/ARTI tracing callback hooks—ArtiTaskSwitch, ArtiTaskWait, ArtiTaskRelease, and ArtiTaskPreempt—used to notify a tracer of task-state changes (switch, wait, ready, pre-empt) on a given core, including whether interrupts may be disabled, with all functions being re-entrant and having no return value.

nodes.title: ArtiTaskRelease
nodes.summary: The excerpt documents three AUTOSAR AP R25-11 OS-tracing hook functions—ArtiTaskWait, ArtiTaskRelease, and ArtiTaskPreempt—declared in “ara/log/osarti.h”. Each reentrant routine is invoked by the OS/ARTI Adapter to signal a specific task-state transition: entering the wait state, leaving wait and becoming ready, and being preempted from running, respectively. All take the same three input parameters (callingContext, coreId, taskId) and return void; no additional behaviour or thread-safety details are provided.

nodes.title: ArtiTaskPreempt
nodes.summary: The excerpt lists six re-entrant “arti” hook functions (ArtiTaskWait, ArtiTaskRelease, ArtiTaskPreempt, ArtiTaskExit, ArtiTaskCreate) that an OS/ARTI Adapter must call to notify the AUTOSAR AP tracer whenever a task changes state: entering wait, leaving wait/returning to ready, being preempted, terminating, or being created. Each function takes a CallingContext plus core/task IDs (and processId for creation), returns void, and is declared in ara/log/osarti.h under upstream requirement RS_OSI_00210.

nodes.title: ArtiTaskExit
nodes.summary: The partial document outlines the main topics and key points discussed within the provided text.

nodes.title: ArtiTaskCreate
nodes.summary:
The excerpt defines four reentrant OS/ARTI tracing hooks that an AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform OS/ARTI Adapter must invoke to report task-lifecycle events to a tracer:

- ArtiTaskExit – signals that an existing task is terminating (parameters: callingContext, coreId, taskId).  
- ArtiTaskCreate – signals that a new task has been created (parameters: callingContext, coreId, processId, taskId).  
- ArtiTaskRename – assigns or updates a human-readable name for a task (parameters: callingContext, taskId, null-terminated taskName).  
- ArtiTaskInfo – supplies complete identification data for a task (parameters: callingContext, taskId, processId, null-terminated taskName).

All functions return void, reside in ara/log/osarti.h, share the upstream requirement RS_OSI_00210, and are thread-safe/reentrant.

nodes.title: ArtiTaskRename
nodes.summary:
The excerpt defines three OS-tracing interface functions that an AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform OS/ARTI Adapter must invoke:

- ArtiTaskCreate – signals to the tracer that a new task has been created.  
- ArtiTaskRename – assigns (or changes) a human-readable, null-terminated name to an existing taskId so the task can be identified in trace logs.  
- ArtiTaskInfo – delivers complete identification data (taskId, owning processId, taskName) for a task to the tracing system.

All three are re-entrant, take a CallingContext argument that indicates interrupt state, return void, and reside in the header “ara/log/osarti.h”.

nodes.title: ArtiTaskInfo
nodes.summary:
The excerpt documents two AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform tracing interface API functions—ArtiTaskRename and ArtiTaskInfo—declared in “ara/log/osarti.h”.  
ArtiTaskRename lets the OS/ARTI Adapter assign a new, null-terminated name to an existing task (identified by taskId) while specifying the interrupt state via callingContext; it is re-entrant and returns nothing.  
ArtiTaskInfo similarly transmits task metadata—taskId, its owning processId, and its null-terminated name—under the same callingContext and thread-safety rules, also returning void.

nodes.title: ArtiProcessSwitch
nodes.summary:
The excerpt defines three OS/ARTI tracing hooks for the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform R25-11:  
1. ArtiTaskInfo – registers an existing task’s name and parent process when tracing starts to populate the initial task list.  
2. ArtiProcessSwitch – notifies the tracer that the CPU has switched to a different process, supplying core ID and new process ID.  
3. ArtiProcessCreate – notifies the tracer that a new process has been created, supplying core ID, new process ID and optional parent process ID.

nodes.title: ArtiProcessCreate
nodes.summary:
The excerpt defines four OS/ARTI tracing hooks that let the adapter report process life-cycle events to the trace collector:  
- ArtiProcessSwitch – announces that a CPU core is changing to another process  
- ArtiProcessCreate – announces the birth of a new process, optionally naming its parent  
- ArtiProcessDestroy – announces that a process is being removed  
- ArtiProcessRename – assigns a new name to an existing process  

Each function is re-entrant, takes a CallingContext flag plus core/process identifiers, returns void, and must be invoked by the OS/ARTI Adapter at the exact moment the corresponding kernel event occurs.

nodes.title: ArtiProcessDestroy
nodes.summary: The excerpt defines three reentrant tracing-hook functions—ArtiProcessDestroy and ArtiProcessRename (plus an earlier ArtiProcessCreate implied by the context)—that an OS/ARTI Adapter must call to notify the AUTOSAR AP tracer when a process is destroyed or renamed. For each hook it lists the upstream requirement, header, prototype, input parameters (callingContext, coreId/processId, optional new name), and states that the functions are thread-safe and return nothing.

nodes.title: ArtiProcessRename
nodes.summary: The excerpt defines four reentrant OS/ARTI Adapter hook functions (ArtiProcessDestroy, ArtiProcessRename, ArtiProcessInfo, ArtiVersionInfo) that reside in ara/log/osarti.h, are invoked with a CallingContext parameter, and serve to notify the tracing layer whenever a process is destroyed, renamed, described, or when version information is exchanged; all return nothing except ArtiVersionInfo, which returns a constant pointer to the ARTI-driver version data.

nodes.title: ArtiProcessInfo
nodes.summary:
The excerpt documents two ARTI API functions—ArtiProcessInfo and ArtiVersionInfo—specified for the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform R25-11 operating-system tracing interface.  
ArtiProcessInfo supplies runtime metadata (process ID, parent ID, null-terminated name) for every existing process; the OS/ARTI Adapter must invoke it once per process immediately after ArtiInit or when tracing starts to populate the initial process list.  
ArtiVersionInfo returns a constant pointer to a constant structure containing the ARTI-driver version information; the caller passes in a constant pointer to the OS version info and receives the driver’s version data in return.  
Both functions are reentrant, take a CallingContext argument that indicates interrupt state, and are declared in “ara/log/osarti.h”.

nodes.title: ArtiVersionInfo
nodes.summary:
The excerpt details four ARTI (AUTOSAR Runtime Tracing Interface) API functions that an OS/ARTI Adapter must call to set up and manage OS-level tracing:

- ArtiProcessInfo – registers an existing process (name, ID, parent) to build the initial process list.  
- ArtiVersionInfo – exchanges version structures between OS and driver; tracing proceeds only if both sides report identical API versions.  
- ArtiInit – initializes the tracing driver once version compatibility is confirmed.  
- ArtiCleanup – performs driver shutdown when tracing ends.

All functions are re-entrant, take a CallingContext parameter indicating interrupt state, and are declared in ara/log/osarti.h.

nodes.title: ArtiInit
nodes.summary:
The excerpt defines three OS/ARTI-Adapter interface functions for AUTOSAR AP R25-11:

1. ArtiVersionInfo – run-time compatibility check: the OS passes its highest supported API version; the ARTI-driver returns its own version.  
   – If both versions match, tracing is enabled.  
   – If OS version is higher, the OS may retry with a lower major version; if still unsupported, tracing is blocked.  
   – If OS version is lower, tracing is impossible.  
   The call is executed once (or twice on retry) immediately before ArtiInit.

2. ArtiInit – initializes the OS/ARTI Adapter; invoked once when the adapter starts, optionally setting up the trace driver.  
   Parameter: callingContext (interrupt state). Re-entrant, no return value.

3. ArtiCleanup – counterpart to ArtiInit; performs adapter shutdown (details cut off in the fragment).

nodes.title: ArtiCleanup
nodes.summary:
The excerpt defines three OS/ARTI Adapter API functions—ArtiVersionInfo, ArtiInit, and ArtiCleanup—specified in AUTOSAR AP R25-11.  
ArtiVersionInfo performs run-time version negotiation: the OS proposes its highest supported API version, the ARTI driver responds with its own, and tracing is enabled only if the versions match or the OS can downgrade; mismatches block tracing.  
ArtiInit initializes the adapter/trace driver at system start-up, while ArtiCleanup releases resources when the adapter is stopped; both are reentrant, take a CallingContext parameter indicating interrupt state, and return nothing.

structure.title: Change History
structure.summary: The partial document outlines the main topics and key points discussed within the provided excerpt.

nodes.title: Change History of this document according to AUTOSAR Release R25-11
nodes.summary: The excerpt is a fragment of the AUTOSAR AP “Operating System Tracing Interface” technical report. It contains only the document’s change-history appendix, listing for releases R25-11 and R24-11 which specification items were added, changed, or deleted. For R25-11 no modifications are recorded, while R24-11 shows ten changed items, all related to ARTI (AUTOSAR Real-Time Interface) task life-cycle and version-info functions.

nodes.title: Added Specification Items in R25-11
nodes.summary:
The partial document is the change-history appendix of the AUTOSAR Technical Report on the Operating-System Tracing Interface (AP R25-11).  
It states that release R25-11 introduced no added, changed, or deleted specification items, while release R24-11 modified ten numbered headings (TR_OSTI_00001 – TR_OSTI_00010) that define ARTI version, initialization/cleanup, and seven task-life-cycle notification points.

nodes.title: Changed Specification Items in R25-11
nodes.summary: The partial document is a technical report on the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform (AP) Operating System Tracing Interface (OSTI) for release R25-11. It contains a detailed change history section, which notes that no specification items were added, changed, or deleted in the R25-11 release. It also includes the change history for the previous release, R24-11, which lists several specification items related to ARTI (Adaptive Runtime Tracing Interface) that were changed, covering aspects such as version info, initialization, cleanup, and various task notifications.

nodes.title: Deleted Specification Items in R25-11
nodes.summary:
The excerpt is a change-history appendix from the AUTOSAR AP R25-11 Technical Report on the Operating-System Tracing Interface (ARTI).  
It lists, release-by-release, which ARTI specification items were added, changed or deleted:

- R25-11: no changes  
- R24-11: 22 items modified (all API definitions for task/process notifications and version-info functions/types)  
- R23-11: same 22 items newly introduced (initial creation)

Thus the fragment only documents the evolution of the ARTI specification across these three AUTOSAR releases.
